title: CENTRALITY DOMINANCE IN CASCADE PROPAGATION
desc: Across tested network cascade models, classical topological degree and betweenness centrality consistently outperform composite Helix operators (SRD, FHO, OGO) in predicting cascade speed and intervention efficacy.
regime: Scale-free, modular, DAG, and trap network structures under static fragmentation and dynamic capacity cascade physics.
falsifier: If a Helix operator consistently exceeds degree/betweenness performance under identical cascade regimes with ≥0.10 predictive uplift, this finding is falsified.

title: TELEMETRY LOSS PREDICTION THRESHOLD
desc: Structural prediction reliability collapses when telemetry dropout approaches approximately 10% in tested network observation regimes.
regime: Graph systems with partial observability under randomized telemetry routing and measurement dropout.
falsifier: If prediction correlation remains stable above 10% telemetry loss in equivalent regimes, the threshold estimate is invalid.

title: SEMANTIC DRIFT DEGRADATION
desc: Predictive correlation decreases approximately linearly with divergence between static structural models and runtime behavior.
regime: Program-graph analysis under simulated AST / runtime mismatch.
falsifier: If systems maintain predictive reliability despite high runtime semantic divergence, this degradation model fails.

title: COMMITMENT GEOMETRY SPLIT
desc: Commitment events exhibit two distinct influence geometries: Funnel geometry Influence concentrates rapidly (`k_eff` decreases). Field geometry Influence distributes broadly (`k_eff` remains high).
regime: Decision processes across optimization, voting, swarm, consensus, and dynamical systems.
falsifier: If systems exhibit commitment events that neither compress nor distribute influence consistently, the geometry split model fails.

title: MINIMAL STRUCTURAL PREDICTORS FOR FUNNEL GEOMETRY
desc: Two architecture variables strongly predict funnel influence geometry: - `centralized_decision = True` - `competition_type = winner_take_all` Systems exhibiting either variable consistently display strong dimensional compression near commitment boundaries.
regime: Optimization, hierarchical voting, and hybrid arbitration architectures.
falsifier: If architectures lacking centralized decision and winner-take-all competition reliably produce funnel geometry, the predictor model fails.
